name: Google Colab
description: Google's free cloud-hosted Jupyter notebook environment with access to GPUs and TPUs. No setup required — open a browser, write Python, and train models on powerful hardware without buying a GPU.
whyUseIt: Free GPU access is Colab's killer feature. Training deep learning models requires a GPU, and Colab gives you one for free (with limits). It is the fastest way to start training models without any local setup or cloud billing.
aiCapabilities: Free GPU (T4) and TPU access for model training. Pre-installed with PyTorch, TensorFlow, and common AI libraries. Integrates with Google Drive for data storage.
engineerWorkflow: An AI engineer uses Colab for GPU-intensive work: training image classifiers, fine-tuning language models, running embedding pipelines on large datasets. For lighter work, they use local Jupyter.
managerExpects: You use Colab for learning and prototyping, not production workloads. For production GPU work, use proper cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP) with cost controls.

vocabulary.definition: The virtual machine that runs your notebook code. You can change the runtime type to include a GPU or TPU.
The free GPU tier in Colab. An NVIDIA T4 with 16GB memory — sufficient for training medium-sized models and fine-tuning with LoRA.

beginnerMistakes: Relying on Colab for production workloads — sessions time out, GPUs are not guaranteed, and data is not persistent
Not saving your work to Google Drive — Colab instances are temporary and data is lost when the session ends
Using the free tier for large training jobs — it has usage limits and will disconnect you mid-training
